topic: What does it mean to support mitochondria?
objective: Create a text-free Nano Banana cover plate for the approved April 6 feed cover
anchor_set: April 6 definition-led cover pacing with a fresh editorial image plate
source_strategy: custom Nano Banana cover plate for design-first compositor assembly
fallback_source: fallback to the approved calmer type-led cover only if the image plate fails the definition-led text-safe-zone or premium-stillness test
visual_intent: quiet intelligence and calm daily-condition reading
fresh image-backed cover with a strong left text-safe zone
more compelling than a plain white cover without becoming dramatic
brand_guardrails: Generate a text-free source plate only.
Keep the image calm, premium, realistic, and medically responsible in tone.
Do not turn the scene into a supplement ad, a biotech visual cliche, or a generic stock wellness image.
Protect the left-side headline zone and the lower three-part band area.
composition: main visual weight anchored in the right half or right-center
clean negative space across the left 38 to 42 percent of the frame for headline overlay
keep the lower 18 to 20 percent quiet enough for the white three-part band overlay
soft morning or daylight interior with restrained editorial calm
reference_strategy: Use the written brief as the primary source of truth and create a fresh editorial image plate with calm intelligence and believable atmosphere rather than a literal science illustration.
variation_guardrails: No readable text, letters, logos, labels, packaging, UI, or watermarks anywhere in frame.
Do not place the focal subject in the left text-safe zone.
Do not let the bottom overlay zone become busy or too dark.
Keep the image definition-led and calm, not dramatic or ad-like.

text_overlay.headline_ja:
ミトコンドリアを
支えるって
どういうこと？

negative_prompts: centered subject blocking the left text area
high-contrast clutter in the lower band zone
notes: Generate only the source image plate for the cover. Final Japanese text and the lower three-part band will be composited later. The image should feel like a calm editorial still that helps the phrase feel more approachable, with the focal subject living on the right half and the left side intentionally protected for copy.
